commit.message:
fix: scope focus-visible outline to buttons and controls only

Resolves #21

The global :focus-visible rule was applying a 2px accent outline to all
focused elements including <select>, <input>, and <textarea>. The
outline-offset: 2px created a floating border that looked like a
strikethrough on dropdowns and text inputs.

Now scoped to: buttons, ctrl-btn, session-tab, tab, dot-close,
bottom-right-btn, floating-btn, picker-item, cappicker-item,
cap-tool-chip, dialog-btn. Form inputs keep their existing
border-color focus styles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update plan document notes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[agentserver] Fix span parenting so framework child spans nest correctly (#46274)

* Fix span parenting so framework child spans nest correctly

Add set_current_span() and detach_context() to core tracing API.
Update _wrap_streaming_response in both responses and invocations
packages to re-attach the OTel span as the current context during
streaming iteration, ensuring child spans created by framework
handlers (e.g. Agent Framework) are correctly parented under the
agentserver invoke_agent span instead of appearing as siblings.

This re-applies the fixes from PRs #46068 and #46069 which were
lost during branch updates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Guard detach_context against out-of-order token detach

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename configure_tracing to configure_observability

Consolidate console logging and tracing setup into a single
overridable function. The default _default_configure_observability
attaches a console StreamHandler to the root logger (so user
logging.info() calls are visible) and configures OTel tracing.

Users can pass configure_observability=None to disable all
SDK-managed observability, or pass a custom function for full
control.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Consolidate observability setup into _tracing.py

Move _default_configure_observability and _CONSOLE_HANDLER_ATTR from
_base.py into _tracing.py so there is a single file for all logging
handlers, tracing exporters, and span operations.

- Renamed: configure_tracing -> _configure_tracing (internal)
- New public: configure_observability (console + tracing + OTel)
- _base.py now just imports and calls _tracing.configure_observability
- Exported configure_observability from core __init__.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract _BaseExchangeAdapter to eliminate ~60% adapter duplication (#22)

* refactor: extract _BaseExchangeAdapter and fix ValidationRunner dead code

Exchange adapters (nordpool.py, epex_spot.py) shared ~60% of their
implementation verbatim. Extract the common behaviour into
_BaseExchangeAdapter in exchanges/base.py:

- capabilities property
- get_products() — 96 QH product IDs using self._area_id prefix
- get_orderbook() — empty OrderBook (matching engine owns state)
- submit_order() / cancel_order() — raise NotImplementedError
- validate_order() — checks ExchangeCapabilities limits, uses
  self._display_name in error messages

NordPoolAdapter and EpexSpotAdapter now inherit _BaseExchangeAdapter and
only define __init__ (ExchangeCapabilities + super().__init__ call) and
gate_closure_offset (which differs between exchanges). Line counts:
nordpool.py 52 -> 24 stmts, epex_spot.py 47 -> 20 stmts.

ValidationRunner.run() pre-created checker instances in the remaining[]
list (all assigned to _checker and immediately discarded), then
re-imported and re-instantiated them inside an if/elif chain. Replace
with Callable[[], StepResult] lambdas — one entry per step, no dead
objects, no duplicated import aliases.

Update GUIDE-NEW-EXCHANGES.md Step 2 template to match the new
inheritance pattern. Add a table of what _BaseExchangeAdapter provides
for free, document the three super().__init__ arguments, and update the
naive-datetime common mistake note (get_orderbook is now in the base).

fix: gRPC URL must use grpcs:// protocol prefix

Without an explicit protocol, Bruno's gRPC event handler prepends
http:// to the URL, causing createInsecure() channel credentials to
be used. CVP on port 443 expects TLS and drops insecure connections
with status 14 UNAVAILABLE.

Change url from {{cvp_host}} to grpcs://{{cvp_host}}:{{cvp_port}} in
all 11 gRPC request files so that TLS credentials are always created
for non-localhost connections.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(grpc): add protoPath to all gRPC request files

Each .bru file now specifies protoPath relative to the collection root.
This tells Bruno to load method definitions from the vendored proto files
instead of attempting gRPC server reflection (which fails against CVP).

Without protoPath, Bruno's startConnection() falls through to reflection,
which CVP does not support, resulting in:
  'Failed to refresh methods and method /X/Y not found'

Proto path mapping:
- inventory/     -> proto/arista/inventory.v1/services.gen.proto
- changecontrol/ -> proto/arista/changecontrol.v1/services.gen.proto
- event/         -> proto/arista/event.v1/services.gen.proto
- lifecycle/     -> proto/arista/lifecycle.v1/services.gen.proto
- tag/           -> proto/arista/tag.v2/services.gen.proto
- workspace/     -> proto/arista/workspace.v1/services.gen.proto

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(grpc): protoFiles paths relative to collection root, not importPaths

Bruno resolves protoFiles relative to the collection directory.
importPaths is used only by the proto compiler for import resolution.

Correct paths: proto/arista/inventory.v1/services.gen.proto
Wrong paths:   arista/inventory.v1/services.gen.proto

commit.message:
Fix expander arrow CSS — cursor, pointer-events, SVG size, markers

The arrow/toggle on st.expander (Geo Refinement, IO Queue) was
unresponsive on Streamlit Cloud. Root cause: global font-family
!important on all elements + missing cursor/pointer-events rules
broke the clickable summary element.

Fixes:
- summary { cursor: pointer } — ensures clickable cursor
- summary svg { pointer-events: none; min-width/height: 16px }
- details { pointer-events: auto } — ensures container is clickable
- ::marker, ::-webkit-details-marker { display: block } — shows arrow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
